items.title: Matplotlib
items.description: Matplotlib owns static Figure/Axes and savefig for publication-ready PNG/PDF. Use Plotly when learners need zoom, hover, and HTML sharing; use Matplotlib when the deliverable is a fixed raster figure in a paper or slide deck.

items.title: Seaborn
items.description: Seaborn provides statistical plotting defaults on Matplotlib for distribution and regression views. Plotly Express offers a parallel DataFrame-oriented API with interactivity—choose Seaborn for static statistical reports, Plotly for exploratory drill-down in notebooks and dashboards.

items.title: Choosing chart types
items.description: That topic decides whether a scatter, line, histogram, or heatmap fits the analytical question; this topic implements the chosen chart as an interactive Plotly figure with appropriate hover encodings.

items.title: Embedding visualization
items.description: Rotatable 3D scatter and rich hover on UMAP/t-SNE coordinates are commonly built with px.scatter_3d or go.Scatter3d here; interpretation guardrails, color semantics, and publication practices for embedding plots live in the embedding visualization topic.
